Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significantly elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, bilirubin, ketones), suggesting impaired renal function and possible nephrotic syndrome or glomerular disease. Dyslipidemia (high cholesterol, LDL, triglycerides) and mildly elevated liver enzymes are present. HbA1c is low, excluding diabetes. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation for renal dysfunction and further assessment of possible underlying causes.
